items.title: Pakistan signs US-Iran MoU

items.content: Pakistan’s Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif signed a US-Iran memorandum of understanding aimed at ending the war.

items.title: US vice president slams Israel over criticism of Trump’s Iran deal
items.standfirst: JD Vance slammed members of Israel’s government for criticising US President Donald Trump over his Iran deal efforts.
items.content: JD Vance slammed members of Israel’s government for criticising US President Donald Trump over his Iran deal efforts.

items.title: Watch: Vance makes case for Iran agreement, reacts to Trump saying he would blame him if deal breaks
items.standfirst: Vice President JD Vance held a press briefing at the White House on Thursday and provided more details on the timing of the Iran deal after President Trump signed the memorandum of understanding with Tehran. CBS News' Aaron Navarro reports.
items.content: Vice President JD Vance held a press briefing at the White House on Thursday and provided more details on the timing of the Iran deal after President Trump signed the memorandum of understanding with Tehran. CBS News' Aaron Navarro reports.

items.title: Trump finally sealed his Iran deal. Now talks on the ‘final’ agreement begin.

items.content: “This wasn’t easy.”That’s the verdict of President Donald Trump, as he finally signed a deal to end the Iran war and reopen the Strait of Hormuz

items.title: Read the full text of Trump's preliminary U.S.-Iran agreement to end the war

items.title: Vance says 60-day negotiating period under draft deal with Iran begins Thursday
items.standfirst: US Vice President JD Vance spoke at a White House press briefing on the interim agreement with Iran on Thursday, saying that some ​12.5 ‌million ⁠barrels ‌of oil have flowed ⁠through the Strait of ​Hormuz following ‌the overnight signing of ‌a memorandum ​of understanding between the United States and ​Iran.
items.content: US Vice President JD Vance spoke at a White House press briefing on the interim agreement with Iran on Thursday, saying that some ​12.5 ‌million ⁠barrels ‌of oil have flowed ⁠through the Strait of ​Hormuz following ‌the overnight signing of ‌a memorandum ​of understanding between the United States and ​Iran.

items.title: Trump signs agreement with Iran. And, the president's approval hits record lows
items.standfirst: Trump signed a preliminary agreement with Iran yesterday to end the war and open the Strait of Hormuz. And, the president's approval rating has hit a record low, according to a new NPR poll.
items.content: Trump signed a preliminary agreement with Iran yesterday to end the war and open the Strait of Hormuz. And, the president's approval rating has hit a record low, according to a new NPR poll.(Image credit: Anna Moneymaker)

items.title: Moment Trump signs US-Iran agreement at Palace of Versailles

items.content: The 14-point Memorandum of Understanding was signed at a post-G7 dinner in France.

items.title: Trump and Iran’s president sign initial deal to end war, open strait and ease sanctions
items.standfirst: The interim agreement declares an intent to bring about an “immediate and permanent termination of military operations” in the U.S.-Israeli war with Iran.
items.content: The interim agreement declares an intent to bring about an “immediate and permanent termination of military operations” in the U.S.-Israeli war with Iran.

clusters.title: Hegseth renews Nato criticism and says US will review presence in Europe
clusters.neutral_headline: Hegseth renews Nato criticism and says US will review presence in Europe
bullet_summary: forces and bases in Europe, Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said Thursday at a meeting of NATO defense ministers
Defence secretary announced decision as he scolded Nato allies for failing to support Washington in Iran war

items.title: Hegseth renews Nato criticism and says US will review presence in Europe
items.standfirst: The US defence secretary's move follows a US decision to scale back its commitments to a high readiness force within the alliance.
items.content: The US defence secretary's move follows a US decision to scale back its commitments to a high readiness force within the alliance.

items.title: Pete Hegseth accuses Nato countries of ‘free riding’ in combative address
items.standfirst: US defence secretary addresses allies in latest attempt to get Europe to raise military budgetsPete Hegseth has announced a review of the US military presence across Europe, in a combative address to Nato allies where he threatened to cut force numbers in countries spending the least on defence.The US defence secretary, speaking at a meeting of Nato defence ministers in Brussels, accused some countries of “free riding” and others of being shameful for not allowing their airbases to be used by US jets bombing Iran in the spring. Continue reading...
items.content: US defence secretary addresses allies in latest attempt to get Europe to raise military budgetsPete Hegseth has announced a review of the US military presence across Europe, in a combative address to Nato allies where he threatened to cut force numbers in countries spending the least on defence.The US defence secretary, speaking at a meeting of Nato defence ministers in Brussels, accused some countries of “free riding” and others of being shameful for not allowing their airbases to be used by US jets bombing Iran in the spring. Continue reading...

items.title: Hegseth announces review of U.S. forces in Europe, blasts "shameful" NATO allies
items.standfirst: The Pentagon is launching a six-month review of U.S. forces and bases in Europe, Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said Thursday at a meeting of NATO defense ministers.
items.content: The Pentagon is launching a six-month review of U.S. forces and bases in Europe, Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said Thursday at a meeting of NATO defense ministers.

items.title: Hegseth unveils six-month review of US military presence in Europe
items.standfirst: Defence secretary announced decision as he scolded Nato allies for failing to support Washington in Iran war
items.content: Defence secretary announced decision as he scolded Nato allies for failing to support Washington in Iran war

items.title: Hegseth lashes out at NATO allies and announces a review of U.S. forces in Europe
items.standfirst: The defense secretary lambasted allies for failing to provide U.S. forces access to bases in Europe to launch attacks on Iran.
items.content: The defense secretary lambasted allies for failing to provide U.S. forces access to bases in Europe to launch attacks on Iran.

clusters.title: Ukraine hits a Moscow oil refinery and other sites in a large-scale drone attack
clusters.neutral_headline: Ukraine hits a Moscow oil refinery and other sites in a large-scale drone attack
bullet_summary: In a post on X, Ukrainian President Volodymyr #Zelensky said #Kyiv also struck targets in #Russia's Rostov region and in the occupied territories of Ukraine
Reported by NPR News, BBC World, France24 and others

items.title: Ukraine hits a Moscow oil refinery and other sites in a large-scale drone attack
items.standfirst: Ukraine launched a wave of drone attacks on Russia early Thursday — one of the largest attacks on Russia's capital since the Kremlin ordered the invasion of Ukraine more than four years ago.
items.content: Ukraine launched a wave of drone attacks on Russia early Thursday — one of the largest attacks on Russia's capital since the Kremlin ordered the invasion of Ukraine more than four years ago.(Image credit: AFP)

items.title: Moscow residents complain of black rain after largest Ukrainian attack hits oil refinery
items.standfirst: A refinery and a shopping centre burned after almost 200 Ukrainian drones struck an area to the south-east of the Russian capital.
items.content: A refinery and a shopping centre burned after almost 200 Ukrainian drones struck an area to the south-east of the Russian capital.

items.title: Ukraine hits Moscow with ‘large-scale’ attack
items.standfirst: 🔴 🇺🇦 🇷🇺 #Ukraine targeted #Moscow's oil refinery in a wave of drone strikes early on Thursday, with the attack wounding at least 17 people. In a post on X, Ukrainian President Volodymyr #Zelensky said #Kyiv also struck targets in #Russia's Rostov region and in the occupied territories of Ukraine.
items.content: 🔴 🇺🇦 🇷🇺 #Ukraine targeted #Moscow's oil refinery in a wave of drone strikes early on Thursday, with the attack wounding at least 17 people. In a post on X, Ukrainian President Volodymyr #Zelensky said #Kyiv also struck targets in #Russia's Rostov region and in the occupied territories of Ukraine.

items.title: Ukraine unleashes biggest attack on Moscow in years, setting a key oil refinery ablaze
items.standfirst: The Russian capital was shrouded in thick black smoke Thursday, after Ukraine launched what appeared to be its largest attack on the city since the Kremlin invaded its neighbor more than four years ago
items.content: The Russian capital was shrouded in thick black smoke Thursday, after Ukraine launched what appeared to be its largest attack on the city since the Kremlin invaded its neighbor more than four years ago

items.title: Ukraine hits Moscow with largest-ever drone attack
items.standfirst: Almost 200 drones strike Russian capital, several damaging the city’s largest oil refinery
items.content: Almost 200 drones strike Russian capital, several damaging the city’s largest oil refinery

clusters.title: Supreme Court sides with Texas man who challenged case on drug use, gun ownership
clusters.neutral_headline: US Supreme Court eases restrictions on drug users owning firearms
bullet_summary: Reported by CBS News, Al Jazeera, Financial Times and others

items.title: Supreme Court sides with Texas man who challenged case on drug use, gun ownership
items.standfirst: The Supreme Court unanimously found that a Texas man's prosecution for having a firearm while he was an occasional, unlawful marijuana user was too broad an interpretation of the law and violated his Second Amendment. CBS News' Jan Crawford reports.
items.content: The Supreme Court unanimously found that a Texas man's prosecution for having a firearm while he was an occasional, unlawful marijuana user was too broad an interpretation of the law and violated his Second Amendment. CBS News' Jan Crawford reports.

items.title: US Supreme Court eases restrictions on drug users owning firearms
items.standfirst: Court sides unanimously with marijuana user who argued law barring him from owning firearms violated US Constitution.
items.content: Court sides unanimously with marijuana user who argued law barring him from owning firearms violated US Constitution.

items.title: Supreme Court sides with man who challenged law barring drug users from having guns
items.standfirst: The Supreme Court on Thursday ruled in favor of a Texas man who challenged a federal law that bars certain drug users from having firearms.
items.content: The Supreme Court on Thursday ruled in favor of a Texas man who challenged a federal law that bars certain drug users from having firearms.

items.title: US Supreme Court narrows law barring drug users from owning guns
items.standfirst: Justices side with a casual marijuana user challenging his ban under legislation used to prosecute Hunter Biden
items.content: Justices side with a casual marijuana user challenging his ban under legislation used to prosecute Hunter Biden

items.title: Supreme Court rules against government in gun case involving drug use
items.standfirst: The Supreme Court sided with a Texas man and ruled against the government after its prosecution of a federal law that bars certain drug users from having firearms. CBS News' Jan Crawford reports.
items.content: The Supreme Court sided with a Texas man and ruled against the government after its prosecution of a federal law that bars certain drug users from having firearms. CBS News' Jan Crawford reports.

items.title: Supreme Court sides with a marijuana user who was barred from owning guns
items.standfirst: The court ruled that the law used to prosecute a marijuana user violated his Second Amendment right to bear arms and is unconstitutionally vague.
items.content: The court ruled that the law used to prosecute a marijuana user violated his Second Amendment right to bear arms and is unconstitutionally vague.(Image credit: Drew Angerer)

clusters.title: Obama opens Chicago presidential center, urges Americans to defend democracy
clusters.neutral_headline: Obama opens Chicago presidential center, urges Americans to defend democracy
bullet_summary: “I hope this center will serve as an affirmation of how precious our democracy truly is,” Obama said, as Stevie Wonder closed the celebration
Speaking at the dedication ceremony, Obama said he hopes the center will affirm "how precious our democracy really is
After more than a decade since it was announced, the Obama Presidential Center and Library officially opens to the public in Chicago's Jackson Park on June 19, 2026

items.title: Obama opens Chicago presidential center, urges Americans to defend democracy
items.standfirst: Former US President Barack Obama opened his presidential center in Chicago on Thursday, calling for the defense of democracy in a star-studded event attended by three former presidents. “I hope this center will serve as an affirmation of how precious our democracy truly is,” Obama said, as Stevie Wonder closed the celebration.
items.content: Former US President Barack Obama opened his presidential center in Chicago on Thursday, calling for the defense of democracy in a star-studded event attended by three former presidents. “I hope this center will serve as an affirmation of how precious our democracy truly is,” Obama said, as Stevie Wonder closed the celebration.

items.title: 'Living celebration of community': Obamas host star-studded opening of Chicago presidential centre
items.standfirst: The campus opens to the public this weekend with a museum, community amenities and more to honour the 44th US president.
items.content: The campus opens to the public this weekend with a museum, community amenities and more to honour the 44th US president.

items.title: Obama Presidential Center grand opening ceremony wraps in Chicago
items.standfirst: The Obama Presidential Center held a star-studded grand opening in Chicago on Thursday. CBS News Chicago's Irika Sargent joins with highlights of the ceremony.
items.content: The Obama Presidential Center held a star-studded grand opening in Chicago on Thursday. CBS News Chicago's Irika Sargent joins with highlights of the ceremony.

items.title: Obama Presidential Center opens in Chicago with star-studded celebrations
items.standfirst: The Obama Presidential Center's grand opening ceremony featured a lineup of tributes from his family and closest colleagues, and musical performances by Bruce Springsteen, The Roots and more.
items.content: The Obama Presidential Center's grand opening ceremony featured a lineup of tributes from his family and closest colleagues, and musical performances by Bruce Springsteen, The Roots and more.

items.title: Former first lady Michelle Obama praises her husband in presidential center dedication speech
items.standfirst: Former first lady Michelle Obama spoke at the dedication ceremony of the Obama Presidential Center in Chicago on Thursday, calling it "an honor" to be by her husband's side. Watch her full speech.
items.content: Former first lady Michelle Obama spoke at the dedication ceremony of the Obama Presidential Center in Chicago on Thursday, calling it "an honor" to be by her husband's side. Watch her full speech.

items.title: Michelle Obama's speech at center opening moves husband to tears
items.standfirst: Former first lady Michelle Obama delivered a loving and heartfelt tribute to her husband at the Obama Presidential Center's grand opening ceremony in Chicago Thursday.
items.content: Former first lady Michelle Obama delivered a loving and heartfelt tribute to her husband at the Obama Presidential Center's grand opening ceremony in Chicago Thursday.

items.title: Watch: Obama speaks at dedication of presidential center in Chicago
items.standfirst: Former President Barack Obama's new presidential center in Chicago held its grand opening on Thursday. Speaking at the dedication ceremony, Obama said he hopes the center will affirm "how precious our democracy really is."
items.content: Former President Barack Obama's new presidential center in Chicago held its grand opening on Thursday. Speaking at the dedication ceremony, Obama said he hopes the center will affirm "how precious our democracy really is."

items.title: Photos show celebrations at Obama Presidential Center opening ceremony in Chicago
items.standfirst: The Obama Presidential Center, museum and library opens in Chicago with a star-studded grand opening ceremony and public watch party on Midway Plaisance.
items.content: The Obama Presidential Center, museum and library opens in Chicago with a star-studded grand opening ceremony and public watch party on Midway Plaisance.

items.title: Explore the Obama Presidential Center and Library opening in Chicago
items.standfirst: After more than a decade since it was announced, the Obama Presidential Center and Library officially opens to the public in Chicago's Jackson Park on June 19, 2026.
items.content: After more than a decade since it was announced, the Obama Presidential Center and Library officially opens to the public in Chicago's Jackson Park on June 19, 2026.

clusters.title: Man charged after toddler ends up in crocodile enclosure at zoo
clusters.neutral_headline: Man charged after toddler ends up in crocodile enclosure at zoo
bullet_summary: Police in eastern England said a man was arrested on suspicion of attempted murder after a 3-year-old boy "ended up in the crocodile enclosure" at a zoo
A three-year-old boy was taken to Addenbrooke's Hospital with critical injuries and is in a stable condition, Cambridgeshire Police said

clusters.title: Gas prices fall below $4 a gallon for the first time in nearly 3 months
clusters.neutral_headline: Gas prices fall below $4 a gallon for the first time in nearly 3 months
bullet_summary: Reported by CBS News, NBC World

items.title: Gas prices fall below $4 a gallon for the first time in nearly 3 months
items.standfirst: The national average for a gallon of regular gas in the U.S. drops to $3.99, the lowest since March 30.
items.content: The national average for a gallon of regular gas in the U.S. drops to $3.99, the lowest since March 30.

items.title: Gas prices fall below $4 for the first time since March
items.standfirst: Gas prices fell below $4 per gallon for the first time since March after more than three months of elevated prices as a result of the Iran war.
items.content: Gas prices fell below $4 per gallon for the first time since March after more than three months of elevated prices as a result of the Iran war.

clusters.title: World Cup 2026: South Africa grab draw with Czechia with late penalty
clusters.neutral_headline: World Cup 2026: South Africa grab draw with Czechia with late penalty

items.title: World Cup 2026: South Africa grab draw with Czechia with late penalty
items.standfirst: Teboho Mokoena equalises through a late penalty to salvage a draw for South Africa in their Group A clash with Czechia.
items.content: Teboho Mokoena equalises through a late penalty to salvage a draw for South Africa in their Group A clash with Czechia.

items.title: World Cup 2026: South Africa fight back to earn draw against Czech Republic
items.standfirst: South Africa kept their World Cup campaign alive on Thursday as Teboho Mokoena's late penalty secured a 1-1 draw against the Czech Republic in Atlanta, denying the Czechs a crucial victory after Michal Sadilek's sixth-minute opener.
items.content: South Africa kept their World Cup campaign alive on Thursday as Teboho Mokoena's late penalty secured a 1-1 draw against the Czech Republic in Atlanta, denying the Czechs a crucial victory after Michal Sadilek's sixth-minute opener.

clusters.title: Waymo recalls robotaxis after some vehicles entered construction zone
clusters.neutral_headline: Waymo recalls robotaxis after some vehicles entered construction zone

items.title: Waymo recalls robotaxis after some vehicles entered construction zone
items.standfirst: The recall follows multiple incidents in which Waymo robotaxis drove past ramp-closure signs and into freeway construction zones.
items.content: The recall follows multiple incidents in which Waymo robotaxis drove past ramp-closure signs and into freeway construction zones.

items.title: Waymo Recalls Robotaxis Over Risk They'll Drive at Speed Into Freeway Construction Zones
items.standfirst: The company's latest recall of 3,871 vehicles follows incidents of its autonomous cars prioritizing other hazards or failing to recognize closed construction zones altogether.
items.content: The company's latest recall of 3,871 vehicles follows incidents of its autonomous cars prioritizing other hazards or failing to recognize closed construction zones altogether.
